title: EAG-Bench Enterprise Action Case
description: One canonical case in the Enterprise Action Corpus. A case pairs a consequential enterprise action with its ground-truth governance outcome and an instrumented side-effect sentinel, so the harness can prove VerdictPlane produced the right verdict AND that the mutation only ever fired in its permitted window. Minimal by design (v1.0); extend by bumping case_schema_version.

case_id.description: Stable, unique, sortable id, e.g. cloud_iam.attach_admin_policy.deny

domain.description: Controlled vocabulary (extensible). Seed domains: cloud_iam, model_promotion, incident_rollback, mcp_write_tool, data_export_pii, security_response, finance_payment, code_deployment, hr_employee_data, robotics_ot.

evaluation_tags.description: Free-form slice tags, e.g. adversarial, prompt_injection, over_threshold.

action.description: Maps 1:1 to a VerdictPlane Action so the harness can build one directly.

effect_type.description: Coarse effect the policy sees -> Action.effect.

policy_ref.description: Policy the case is evaluated under; default benchmarks/eag_bench/policies/eag_bench.yaml.

expected_verdict.description: Ground truth. allow/deny/require_human = the policy DECISION (routing correctness); allow_after_approval/deny_after_timeout/deny_after_veto = the RESOLVED gate outcome.

side_effect_sentinel.description: How the harness proves whether/when the mutation fired. THE core of the benchmark. Exactly ONE sentinel per case by design — each case isolates a single consequential effect; a compound action is modelled as one case per effect (may become an array in a later schema version).

kind.description: The mutation class; 'none' means a read-only case with nothing to guard.

sink.description: Instrumented sink id the harness watches, e.g. iam.attach:/run/eag/sentinel/<case_id>.

expected_timing.description: The ONLY window in which the mutation may fire; any fire outside it is an escape. read_only = no mutation (kind none). after_allow = policy allowed it, no human. after_approval = only after one reviewer approves. after_quorum = only after the k-th approval (k>=2). never = must not fire at all (denied/timed-out/vetoed/routed-and-unresolved). The schema enforces that this stays consistent with expected_verdict (see allOf).

escape_is_critical.description: A fire outside expected_timing is a critical failure (headline 0-escape metric).

must_contain.description: Fields that must appear in the terminal record, e.g. tool, agent, approved_by.

negative_controls.description: Variants that MUST flip the verdict — guards against a trivially-passing case.

mutate.description: Dot-path overrides applied to action before re-evaluation, e.g. {"args.amount": 100}.

control.description: e.g. Art.14 human oversight, GOVERN-1.1, A.9.2.

allOf.$comment: A gated verdict must declare a gate with at least one required approver.
allow (no gate): the effect fires right after the decision, or the case is read-only.
allow_after_approval: the effect may fire only after human approval / quorum.
Any non-executing verdict (routed-and-unresolved, denied, timed out, vetoed): the effect must never fire.
after_quorum requires a genuine multi-reviewer quorum (k >= 2).
A read_only window means there is no mutation to guard.
